purpose: Determine whether MCP load control and proactive collaboration-topology/context checkpoints require a repository-authored Skill or Hook after native and current-chain capabilities are exhausted.
observedDemands: many default-enabled MCP servers can add startup, process, tool-list, authentication, and context overhead
AGENTS.md or rules can request proactive branch, worktree, handoff, and context-efficiency behavior but cannot itself prove that runtime signals or actuation are available
automatic context compaction does not by itself prove operation inside a preferred efficiency interval

codexLocalBaseline.evidence: mcp list exposed standalone, runtime, plugin, and remote entries, while mcp get could not address sampled runtime/plugin-visible names

openAiOfficialSourceBaseline.relationshipToInstalledVersion: current official source baseline, not asserted as the exact source revision of installed 0.144.5
verifiedNativeControls: enabled_tools and disabled_tools bound the advertised tool set
PluginMcpServerConfig.enabled=false skips one plugin-provided MCP server
plugin MCP policy owns enablement and tool policy while the plugin manifest owns transport
openAiOfficialSourceBaseline.profileFinding: the current ConfigProfile type does not contain mcp_servers or plugins, so named profile selection is not yet proven as the complete MCP-set switch

currentJudgment.recommendedCurrentRoute: prefer native startup enablement and tool filtering first; treat Skill as task-to-capability decision logic and Hook as optional event observation or startup preparation only after a verified actuation surface exists

portableRepositoryVersion.role: product-neutral upstream collaboration invariants, dynamic checkpoint contracts, Hook modes, verification, and safe degradation

privateSelfUseVersion.role: personal Codex paths, proactive topology preferences, context policy, real Hook wiring, verification, and rollback

publicConsumerVersion.futureAllowedForm: sanitized generated template or projection with explicit provenance and non-authority wording
publicConsumerVersion.reason: avoid three manually drifting authorities while preserving public reuse

candidateSignals: task intent phase and expected duration
context usage compaction or handoff event when exposed by the runtime
user override and non-waivable authority floor
prohibitions: no silent persistent MCP or plugin reconfiguration
no process kill as a substitute for a supported lifecycle contract
no context threshold presented as exact without runtime telemetry
no automatic branch worktree commit push or cleanup beyond current authority
no duplicated manually maintained public and private authority

nextGate: Build disposable startup matrices that compare MCP-disabled, tool-filtered, plugin-MCP-disabled, and baseline launches; separately inspect runtime context/compaction event surfaces. Author only after a reproducible residual gap remains and a safe actuation/rollback contract exists.
nonActions: no MCP, Plugin, App, Hook, AGENTS, rules, or Codex configuration write
no server process termination or restart
no codex-user-config private or public repository write
no repository-authored Skill or Hook implementation
